cases.title: Webster & Fowler v. Heritage Presbytery

parties.complainant_or_appellant: RE Morris Webster and RE Wayne Fowler

topics: fundamentals of the system of doctrine
cases.synopsis: RE Webster and RE Fowler complained that Heritage Presbytery exceeded its authority by passing a motion cautioning the Session of New Covenant Presbyterian Church about allowing a candidate potentially unqualified on infant baptism to stand for election to office. The SJC denied the complaint, finding that Presbytery's motion constituted permissible advice to Session rather than a directive, and did not impinge on Session's or the congregation's prerogatives.

cases.title: Dailey v. Heritage Presbytery
cases.synopsis: RE Dailey complained that Heritage Presbytery erred in upholding its Moderator's ruling that a church member's document was administratively in order as a complaint under BCO 43-3, after the Session had ruled it out of order. The SJC sustained the complaint, holding that neither triggering condition of BCO 43-3 was met because the Session's ruling that the document did not qualify as a BCO 43-1 complaint was not equivalent to denying it, and the Session had considered and responded to the matter in a timely manner. A substantial dissent argued for a broader interpretation of 'fails to consider' in BCO 43-3.

cases.title: Postles v. Heritage Presbytery
precedent_refs: Malone v. Metropolitan New York Presbytery (SJC 2007-02)
cases.synopsis: RE Postles complained that Heritage Presbytery erred in acting on an ad interim committee recommendation to charge a teaching elder with violating ordination vows 6 and 7 and appointing a prosecutor. The SJC denied the complaint, finding that a committee may recommend judicial action without special authorization (distinguishing from a commission under BCO 15-3), that BCO 31-2 procedures were properly followed, and that the language used by Presbytery in its motion was constitutionally sufficient to institute process.

cases.title: Clements v. Blue Ridge Presbytery (Reference and Complaint)
cases.synopsis: Blue Ridge Presbytery requested a reference for the SJC to hear and decide TE Clements' complaint (case 2017-07), but the SJC denied the request for reference, finding the record did not demonstrate sufficient bias, constitutional questions, or unusual difficulty to justify reference under BCO 41-2 or 41-3, directing BRP to hear and decide the complaint itself. Case 2017-07 (the companion complaint) was answered by reference to this decision.

cases.title: Clements v. Blue Ridge Presbytery (Complaint)
cases.synopsis: Companion complaint to 2017-06; the SJC answered this complaint by reference to its decision denying the request for reference in 2017-06, directing Blue Ridge Presbytery to hear and decide the matter.

cases.title: In Re Korean Eastern Presbytery
cases.synopsis: The SJC Commission recognized that Korean Eastern Presbytery complied with its citation, provided required materials under BCO 40-1, and referred those materials to the Committee for Review of Presbytery Records.

cases.title: In Re Korean Southwest Presbytery
cases.synopsis: The SJC Commission unanimously accepted responses from Korean Southwest Presbytery as complying with the SJC citation and forwarded those responses to the Review of Presbytery Records Committee.

cases.title: In Re Platte Valley Presbytery
cases.synopsis: The SJC Commission unanimously accepted responses from Platte Valley Presbytery as complying with the SJC citation and forwarded those responses and requested minutes to the Review of Presbytery Records Committee.
